question.text: On the SME exchange, what is the maximum post-issue paid-up capital for a company to remain listed without migrating to the main board?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** On the SME exchange, the standard threshold for post-issue paid-up capital is Rs. 10 crore. However, companies can choose to stay on the SME exchange with capital up to Rs. 25 crore. Beyond Rs. 25 crore, migration to the main board becomes necessary. **The Trap:** Rs. 10 crore is the default SME threshold but not the maximum — companies can choose to remain up to Rs. 25 crore. Candidates who remember only the basic Rs. 10 crore limit will miss the nuance. **Cross-Exam Context:** SME exchange rules are tested in NISM Series IX. NISM Series XII may reference SME exchanges as part of market structure. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Think of it as two tiers — Rs. 10 crore is the default SME ceiling, but companies can elect to stay up to Rs. 25 crore. Above Rs. 25 crore, main board migration is mandatory.
